title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best Haemul Pajeon Recipe at home! This authentic Korean seafood pancake is crispy, savory, and packed with fresh seafood. Perfect for a delicious meal.
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon Recipe (Korean Seafood Pancake)
h2Headings: How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Guide
Tips for the Best Crispy Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A freshly cooked Haemul Pajeon, a crispy Korean seafood pancake, served with a dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a delicious homemade Haemul Pajeon, a savory Korean seafood pancake, perfect as an appetizer or light meal!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, celebrated for its irresistible crispy edges and tender, flavorful interior. Packed with an abundance of fresh seafood like shrimp and squid, combined with vibrant green onions, it offers a delightful textural contrast. This traditional dish is perfect as a comforting meal or a fantastic appetizer, especially when paired with a tangy dipping sauce.

ingredients.notes: cleaned, cut into rings or bite-sized pieces
for frying, plus more as needed
Korean chili flakes, for dipping sauce, optional

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, water, sugar, toasted sesame seeds, and gochugaru (if using). Set aside while you prepare the pancake.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, salt, and beaten egg. Gradually add the cold water, whisking until a smooth, lump-free batter forms. Be careful not to overmix; a few small lumps are fine.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Ensure your shrimp and squid are cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces. Slice the green onions into 3-4 inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and red chili pepper.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients for Pancake
instructions.text: Gently fold the chopped shrimp, squid, sliced yellow onion, and red chili pepper (if using) into the prepared batter. Do not add the green onions to the batter yet.

instructions.title: Cook the First Side of the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, arrange a portion of the green onion lengths evenly in the pan. Pour about 1/4 to 1/3 of the batter mixture over the green onions, spreading it thinly to form a round pancake. Cook for 4-6 minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Cook the Second Side
instructions.text: Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pancake. Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. Press down gently with the spatula to ensure even contact with the pan. Cook for another 4-6 minutes, or until the second side is golden brown, crispy, and the seafood is fully cooked through.

instructions.title: Repeat and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Repeat the cooking process with the remaining batter and green onions, adding more oil as needed for each pancake. Slice the finished pancakes into wedges and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For extra crispiness, use a combination of all-purpose flour, rice flour, and cornstarch in your batter. Using very cold water also helps achieve a lighter, crispier texture.
Do not overcrowd the pan. Cook Haemul Pajeon in batches to ensure each pancake gets perfectly crispy. Adding green onions directly to the hot pan before the batter helps them caramelize slightly.

Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan. Serve immediately with the dipping sauce. Leftovers can be reheated in a dry skillet over medium heat until crispy again, though the texture may not be quite the same.
article:
**Haemul Pajeon: Unveiling the Crispy, Savory Delight of Korean Seafood Pancake**

There are few dishes that capture the heart and soul of Korean comfort food quite like Haemul Pajeon. This iconic savory pancake, brimming with fresh seafood and aromatic green onions, offers a delightful symphony of textures and flavors. With its irresistibly crispy edges and tender, savory interior, Haemul Pajeon is a beloved staple enjoyed in bustling street markets, cozy homes, and elegant restaurants across Korea and increasingly, around the world. Whether you're seeking a hearty snack, a delightful appetizer, or a centerpiece for a casual gathering, this Korean seafood pancake promises a culinary experience that is both engaging and deeply satisfying. Let's dive into what makes Haemul Pajeon a must-try dish and how you can master its creation at home.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

At its core, Haemul Pajeon is a type of "jeon," a general term for Korean pan-fried dishes made with various ingredients coated in a batter. The name itself offers a clear description: "Haemul" means seafood, and "Pa" refers to green onions (scallions). So, Haemul Pajeon literally translates to "seafood green onion pancake." What sets it apart from other jeon varieties is the generous amount of green onions, often laid out almost like a bed, and the medley of fresh seafood nestled within a light, flavorful batter.

Unlike some thicker, doughier pancakes, a well-made Haemul Pajeon boasts a delicate balance – incredibly crispy on the outside, thanks to careful frying, yet soft and moist within, allowing the natural sweetness of the seafood and the freshness of the green onions to shine through. It's often associated with rainy days in Korea, perfectly paired with a chilled glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine), creating a cozy and convivial atmosphere.

## Ingredients for Haemul Pajeon

Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon requires a thoughtful selection of fresh ingredients. The beauty of this dish lies in its flexibility, allowing you to customize the seafood to your preference. Here's what you'll typically need for a delicious Haemul Pajeon Recipe:

**For the Batter:**
*   **All-purpose flour:** The base of your batter.
*   **Rice flour or cornstarch:** Crucial for achieving that coveted crispy texture.
*   **Egg:** Adds richness and helps bind the batter.
*   **Cold water or anchovy broth:** Using cold liquid helps create a crispier pancake by inhibiting gluten development.
*   **Salt:** For seasoning the batter.

**For the Fillings:**
*   **Green onions (scallions):** The star of the show! You'll need a generous bunch, cut into long segments.
*   **Assorted seafood:** A mix is best. Common choices include small shrimp (peeled and deveined), squid (calamari rings or strips), mussels, and oysters. You can use fresh or thawed frozen seafood. Ensure it's well-drained and patted dry.
*   **Other vegetables (optional):** Thinly sliced onion, red bell pepper, or a few slices of green chili for a touch of heat and color.

**For Frying:**
*   **Vegetable oil:** You'll need a good amount for pan-frying to achieve that golden crisp.

Having these ingredients ready will set you up for success when following your Haemul Pajeon Recipe.

## How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Guide

Making Haemul Pajeon is a rewarding experience that yields incredible results. Follow this step-by-step Haemul Pajeon Recipe for a delectable homemade version:

**Step 1: Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables**
Clean and prepare your seafood. If using shrimp, peel and devein. Cut squid into bite-sized rings or strips. Pat all seafood thoroughly dry with paper towels to prevent a soggy pancake. Cut green onions into 3-4 inch lengths. If using other vegetables like onion or bell pepper, slice them thinly.

**Step 2: Make the Batter**
In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour (or cornstarch), salt, and egg. Gradually add the very cold water or anchovy broth, whisking until just combined. A few lumps are perfectly fine; overmixing can lead to a tough pancake. The batter should be the consistency of a thin pancake batter, easily pourable but not watery.

**Step 3: Combine Ingredients**
Gently fold the prepared seafood and any additional vegetables into the batter. Try not to break up the green onions too much.

**Step 4: Pan-Fry the Pancake**
Heat a large, non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of vegetable oil, enough to coat the bottom of the pan and lightly fry the pancake (about 2-3 tablespoons per pancake). Once the oil is hot and shimmering, pour about 1 cup of the batter mixture into the pan. Use a spatula to spread it evenly into a thin, round pancake, ensuring the green onions and seafood are distributed throughout.

**Step 5: Cook and Flip**
Let the pancake cook undisturbed for 3-5 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and crispy and the bottom is set. You can drizzle a little more oil around the edges to encourage crispiness. Carefully slide your spatula underneath the pancake and flip it over. Cook the second side for another 3-5 minutes, or until golden brown and cooked through. Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil as needed for each pancake.

## Tips for the Best Crispy Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that perfect crisp exterior while maintaining a tender interior is key to an outstanding Haemul Pajeon. Here are some essential tips to elevate your Haemul Pajeon Recipe:

*   **Use Cold Liquid:** As mentioned, using ice-cold water or broth in your batter helps create a crispier pancake by reducing gluten development.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter:** A slightly lumpy batter is better than an overmixed one, which can result in a tough pancake.
*   **Plenty of Oil:** Don't be shy with the oil! This is a pan-fried dish, not a sauté. Sufficient oil ensures a beautiful golden crust.
*   **Hot Pan, Then Medium Heat:** Start with a hot pan to get an immediate sizzle and crisp, then reduce to medium to ensure the inside cooks thoroughly without burning the outside.
*   **Thin is In:** Aim for thinner pancakes. Thicker pancakes tend to be doughier, while thinner ones maximize the crispy surface area.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry:** Excess moisture from the seafood can make your pancake soggy. Always pat your seafood dry with paper towels before adding it to the batter.
*   **Drizzle Oil During Cooking:** After flipping, drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake. This helps create extra crispy edges.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce

Haemul Pajeon is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways. It makes a fantastic appetizer, a satisfying snack, or even a light main course.

**The Essential Dipping Sauce:** No Haemul Pajeon is complete without its accompanying dipping sauce. A simple yet flavorful sauce can be made by combining:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional for heat)
*   1 teaspoon finely chopped green onion

Mix all ingredients together and serve alongside the warm pancakes.

**Pairings:** Traditionally, Haemul Pajeon is often served with makgeolli, the milky Korean rice wine, especially on a rainy day. It also pairs wonderfully with soju, beer, or a refreshing Korean barley tea. It can be part of a larger Korean meal, served alongside kimchi, other banchan (side dishes), and a bowl of rice.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Haemul Pajeon Recipe is a masterpiece, feel free to experiment and adapt it to your taste or dietary needs:

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan Pajeon:** Omit the seafood and egg. You can substitute with thinly sliced mushrooms (shiitake, oyster), zucchini, carrots, or even tofu. For a vegan version, use a flax egg or simply omit the egg and adjust liquid slightly.
*   **Kimchi Pajeon:** For a spicy, tangy kick, add chopped kimchi to your batter. This is another incredibly popular Korean pancake variety.
*   **Meat Options:** While less common for "pajeon," you can add thinly sliced beef or pork belly for a heartier pancake.
*   **Different Seafood:** Feel free to use scallops, clams, or even imitation crab meat if that's what you have on hand. The beauty of this Haemul Pajeon Recipe is its adaptability.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a pancake; it's a celebration of fresh ingredients, satisfying textures, and the rich culinary heritage of Korea. By following this detailed guide and embracing the tips for crispiness, you'll be able to create an authentic and incredibly delicious Haemul Pajeon Recipe that will impress friends and family alike. So, gather your ingredients, fire up your pan, and get ready to enjoy a truly delightful Korean culinary experience!
